key_insight: SrTiO3-xHx oxyhydrides strongly favor the cis O4H2 anion arrangement, and this ordering can only be reliably captured when the DFT+U treatment is calibrated against HSE06.
engineering_relevance: Accurate anion-order predictions are essential for designing oxyhydrides with targeted stability and transport properties, especially at non-dilute hydrogen contents.
method: Built a cluster expansion for SrTiO3-xHx using HSE06 reference energetics.
Benchmarked DFT+U settings against hybrid-DFT and identified U = 3.3 eV as an HSE06 proxy.
Used ground-state searches and Monte Carlo sampling to map short- and long-range anion order.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports DFT+U sensitivity of up to about 100 meV per anion and a cis stabilization of roughly 200 meV per hydride, supported by ground-state, correlation, and Monte Carlo results.
watch_item: The ordering conclusions depend strongly on the chosen U value, so transferring the model to other compositions or oxides may require revalidation with higher-level reference calculations.
paper_title: Long- and Short-Range Anion Order in SrTiO$_{3-x}$H$_x$ Perovskite Oxyhydrides: DFT+$U$ Sensitivity and HSE06 Cluster Expansion
